picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks are on an impressive 9-game winning streak and have dominated this Eastern Conference Finals series, leading 3-0 with three double-digit victories. They boast a 9-1 ATS record in their last 10 games and have covered the spread in every road game this postseason. A significant 4-point line movement from Cavaliers -1.5 to Knicks -2.5 indicates strong sharp money backing New York. Given their current form and ATS success, the Knicks are well-positioned to cover this spread and complete the sweep.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: Two of the three games in this series have already gone over the total, with scores like 115-104 and 121-108. The Knicks' offense has been highly efficient, shooting over 55% from the field in Game 3 and averaging 120.9 points in their last 10 games. Cleveland's coaching strategy has reportedly de-emphasized defensive identity, opting for offensive-minded lineups, which contributes to higher-scoring games. With New York's strong offensive rhythm and Cleveland's need to push the pace in an elimination game, the Over is a strong play.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks are on the verge of sweeping the Eastern Conference Finals, holding a commanding 3-0 series lead and having won 10 consecutive playoff games by an average of 22.5 points. Their superior offensive rhythm and consistent late-game execution have been evident throughout the series. The moneyline has seen significant movement in favor of the Knicks, from -115 to -145, reflecting strong confidence from bettors. Considering their dominant form and the Cavaliers' struggles, a straight-up win for New York is highly probable.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: Josh Hart has consistently exceeded this combined points and rebounds line in every game of the current series, posting totals of 20, 30, and 21. He is an active player for the New York Knicks. Hart's rebounding opportunities have been exceptionally high, and he is also taking a significant volume of shots, as the Cavaliers' defense is allowing him to shoot. This consistent high volume in both categories makes the Over a confident pick for his performance in Game 4.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: Donovan Mitchell is the Cleveland Cavaliers' leading scorer, averaging 25.7 points through 17 playoff games. He is an active player for the Cleveland Cavaliers. Facing elimination in Game 4, Mitchell is expected to play with heightened desperation and has a history of performing well in previous elimination games this postseason. Despite a lower-scoring Game 3, he maintains a high shot volume, averaging 21.2 attempts per game in the playoffs, ensuring ample scoring opportunities.

The Carolina Hurricanes, with a 53-22-7 record, are the stronger team and hold an impressive 9-1 postseason record, including being unbeaten on the road. Despite a close 3-2 overtime victory in Game 2, Carolina dominated Montreal in underlying statistics, controlling shots and pace. With Patrik Laine sidelined for the Canadiens, Carolina's robust defensive structure and superior puck control are expected to give them a decisive edge in this pivotal Game 3. The slight line movement favoring Carolina further suggests sharp money is backing them to win outright.

After a high-scoring Game 1, Game 2 reverted to a tighter 3-2 overtime decision, which is more indicative of Carolina's strong defensive identity. The Hurricanes effectively stifled Montreal's offense, limiting them to just 12 shots in Game 2. With Patrik Laine out due to injury for the Canadiens, their offensive capabilities are reduced, further supporting a lower-scoring contest. The plus-money odds on the Under 5.5 provide excellent value, especially if Carolina maintains its disciplined defensive play and controls the game's tempo.

Historically, the Montreal Canadiens have proven adept at keeping games close against the Carolina Hurricanes, covering the spread in 5 of their last 6 head-to-head matchups. This trend suggests that even if they lose, it's often by a single goal, which is a common occurrence in intense playoff series. As Game 3 shifts to Montreal with the series tied 1-1, the Canadiens will be highly motivated to defend their home ice. Betting on Montreal +1.5 at -220 offers a relatively safe play given their consistent ability to stay competitive against Carolina.

picks.pick: Nikolaj Ehlers Over 2.5 Shots on Goal
picks.rationale: Nikolaj Ehlers has been a prominent offensive force for the Carolina Hurricanes, highlighted by his overtime game-winner in Game 2 and an impressive average of 6.11 shot attempts per game this postseason. Carolina's consistent puck possession and significant offensive zone time, particularly evident in their Game 2 performance, create numerous opportunities for Ehlers to direct shots on net. Given his active status and high shot volume, betting on him to exceed 2.5 shots on goal at -105 odds represents a strong value proposition.

Logan Stankoven has emerged as a crucial offensive contributor for the Carolina Hurricanes in the postseason, leading the team in both expected goals (5) and actual goals (7). He plays a significant role as the second-line center and on the second power-play unit, maximizing his opportunities for point production. With the Hurricanes expected to control much of the play, Stankoven is well-positioned to capitalize, making the plus-money odds (+105) on him to record at least one point an attractive and high-confidence pick. Stankoven is active and playing for the Hurricanes today.

picks.game: St. Louis Cardinals vs Milwaukee Brewers
picks.rationale: The Milwaukee Brewers hold a significant pitching advantage with Jacob Misiorowski (1.89 ERA) on the mound, who has delivered four consecutive scoreless outings and leads the NL in strikeouts. In contrast, Cardinals' starter Matthew Liberatore carries a 4.70 ERA and has allowed four or more runs in his last two starts. The Brewers are also playing at home, where they boast a 16-11 record, and are looking for a bounce-back win after recent losses.

picks.game: St. Louis Cardinals vs Milwaukee Brewers
picks.rationale: Jacob Misiorowski, the Brewers' starting pitcher, has been exceptional this season, ranking in the 98th percentile in whiff percentage and 99th percentile in strikeout percentage. He has surpassed 8.5 strikeouts in six of his ten starts this year, including two of his last three outings. Facing a Cardinals lineup that, despite a low overall strikeout rate, has a higher strikeout rate against right-handed pitching, Misiorowski's dominant 99.7 MPH fastball should lead to another high-strikeout performance.

The Houston Astros are significantly hampered by injuries to key offensive players, with Carlos Correa, Jose Altuve, and Yainer Diaz on the injured list, and Yordan Alvarez day-to-day after leaving the May 23rd game with back spasms. While the Texas Rangers are also dealing with injuries to Corey Seager and Josh Jung (day-to-day), the Astros' lineup is more severely depleted. Playing at home, the Rangers have an advantage against a struggling Astros offense.

The Cleveland Guardians, leading their division, have a strong pitching staff with Gavin Williams (7 wins, 84 strikeouts) and Parker Messick (2.24 ERA, 70 strikeouts) contributing significantly. The Washington Nationals, conversely, are facing substantial pitching injuries, notably with Jake Irvin on the 15-day IL due to a shoulder strain after exiting his last game. The Guardians' depth, as demonstrated by Kyle Manzardo's clutch hitting, further strengthens their position at home against an injury-plagued Nationals team.

picks.game: Colorado Rockies vs Los Angeles Dodgers
picks.rationale: The Los Angeles Dodgers are strong favorites at home against a Colorado Rockies team severely impacted by injuries, particularly to their pitching rotation, including Chase Dollander and Ryan Feltner. The Rockies' overall record of 20-34 reflects their struggles, which are exacerbated by these absences. While the Dodgers have some injuries, they are bolstered by the expected return of Kiké Hernández, adding depth and versatility to their lineup against a weakened opponent.

playerProps.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
playerProps.rationale: Jarrett Allen has consistently exceeded this point total, hitting the over in 5 of his last 6 games and 7 of his last 9. He is an efficient scorer from the interior and frequently gets to the free-throw line, making this a favorable matchup for him to continue his scoring trend.

playerProps.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
playerProps.rationale: Josh Hart demonstrated strong rebounding in Game 3 of this series and maintained an average of 8.8 rebounds in the preceding series. He has also consistently cleared his combined points and rebounds line in every game of this series, indicating high volume and opportunity on the boards.

playerProps.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
playerProps.rationale: Evan Mobley has been a consistent offensive contributor for the Cavaliers against the Knicks in this postseason, averaging 17.7 points per game in the series. His performance at home has been even stronger, with an average of 18.8 points on 61.5% shooting during the 2026 NBA Playoffs.

playerProps.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
playerProps.rationale: Jalen Brunson has been the primary offensive engine for the Knicks, consistently delivering strong scoring performances. He scored 30 points in the last outing and typically shoulders a significant portion of the scoring load, especially in road games.

playerProps.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
playerProps.rationale: OG Anunoby has been increasingly productive, contributing 21 points and 7 rebounds in Game 3, which aligns with his postseason average of 20.5 points + rebounds per game. He has been even more effective recently, averaging 23.5 points + rebounds over his last six contests.
